summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Mineral Identification
summary_sections.content_html:
Minerals are naturally occurring, inorganic solids with a definite chemical composition and crystalline structure.

  • Hardness: resistance to scratching, measured by Mohs Hardness Scale (1 = talc, 10 = diamond)
  • Luster: how a mineral reflects light (metallic vs. non-metallic: glassy, waxy, pearly, earthy)
  • Streak: the color of a mineral's powder when rubbed on an unglazed porcelain plate; more reliable than surface color
  • Cleavage: tendency to break along flat planes of weakness
  • Fracture: irregular breaking pattern (conchoidal, fibrous, etc.)
  • Specific gravity: ratio of a mineral's density to water's density
